sections.label: Section 1132
sections.original: ἔα ·
sections.translation: Ha ! these are heartless words , unwarranted suspicions !

sections.label: Section 1133
sections.original: ἔχʼ ἥσυχος .
sections.translation: Peace ! answer me that question first .

sections.label: Section 1134
sections.original: σὺ δʼ , ἤν γʼ ἐρωτᾷς εἰκότʼ , εἰκότʼ ἂν κλύοις .
sections.translation: Put a fair question and you shall have a fair answer .

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: οὐκ ἄλλʼ ἐρωτῶ , καὶ σὺ μὴ λέγʼ ἄλλα μοι .
sections.translation: I have no other questions to put ; give me no other answers .

sections.label: Section 1136
sections.original: ὦ πότνια μοῖρα καὶ τύχη δαίμων τʼ ἐμός .
sections.translation: O fate revered , O destiny , and my fortune !

sections.label: Section 1137
sections.original: κἀμός γε καὶ τῆσδʼ , εἷς τριῶν δυσδαιμόνων .
sections.translation: Yes , and mine and hers too ; the three share one bad fortune .

sections.label: Section 1138
sections.original: τίνʼ ἠδίκησαι ;
sections.translation: Whom have I injured ?

sections.label: Section 1138b
sections.original: τοῦτʼ ἐμοῦ πεύθῃ πάρα ;
sections.translation: Do you ask this question ? A thought like that itself amounts to tboughtlessness .

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: ἀπωλόμεσθα . προδέδοται τὰ κρυπτά μου .
sections.translation: Ruined ! my secret betrayed !

sections.label: Section 1141
sections.original: πάντʼ οἶδα , καὶ πεπύσμεθʼ ἃ σὺ μέλλεις με δρᾶν ·
sections.translation: I know all ; I have heard what you are bent on doing to me . [*] Your very silence and those frequent groans are a confession ; do not tire yourself by telling it .

sections.label: Section 1144
sections.original: ἰδοὺ σιωπῶ · τὸ γὰρ ἀναίσχυντον τί δεῖ
sections.translation: See , I am silent ; for why should I tell you a falsehood ,

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: ψευδῆ λέγοντα προσλαβεῖν τῇ συμφορᾷ ;
sections.translation: and add effrontery to misfortune ?

sections.label: Section 1146
sections.original: ἄκουε δή νυν · ἀνακαλύψω γὰρ λόγους ,
sections.translation: Well , now listen ; for I will unfold my meaning and no longer employ dark riddles . In the first place — to reproach you first with this — it was not of my own free will but by force that you took and wed me ,

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: τὸν πρόσθεν ἄνδρα Τάνταλον κατακτανών ·
sections.translation: after slaying Tantalus , my former husband , and dashing [*] my baby on the ground when you had torn him from my breast with brutal violence . Then those two sons of Zeus , who were my brothers , came flashing on horseback to war with you ;

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: πατὴρ δὲ πρέσβυς Τυνδάρεώς σʼ ἐρρύσατο
sections.translation: but Tyndareus , my old father , rescued you because of your suppliant prayers , and you in turn had me to wife . Once I was reconciled to you upon this footing , you will bear me witness I have been a blameless wife to you and your family , chaste in love ,

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: μέλαθρον αὔξουσʼ , ὥστε σʼ εἰσιόντα τε
sections.translation: an honor to your house , that so your coming in might be with joy and your going out with gladness . And it is seldom a man secures a wife like this , though the getting of a worthless woman Is no rarity .

sections.label: Section 1164
sections.original: τίκτω δʼ ἐπὶ τρισὶ παρθένοισι παῖδά σοι
sections.translation: Besides three daughters , of one of whom you are heartlessly depriving me ,

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: τόνδʼ , ὧν μιᾶς σὺ τλημόνως μʼ ἀποστερεῖς .
sections.translation: I am the mother of this son of yours . If anyone asks you your reason for slaying her , tell me , what will you say ? or must I say it for you ? It is that Menelaus may recover Helen . An honorable exchange , indeed , to pay a wicked woman ’ s price in children ’ s lives !

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: τἄχθιστα τοῖσι φιλτάτοις ὠνούμεθα .
sections.translation: It is buying what we most detest [*] with what we hold most dear . Again , if you go forth with the army , leaving me in your halls and are long absent at Troy , what will my feelings be at home , do you think ? when I behold each vacant chair

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: κενοὺς δὲ παρθενῶνας , ἐπὶ δὲ δακρύοις
sections.translation: and her chamber now deserted , and then sit down alone in tears , making ceaseless lamentation for her , Ah ! my child , he that begot you has slain you himself , he and no one else , nor are was it by another ’ s hand , [*] leaving behind him such a return to his home .

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: ἐπεὶ βραχείας προφάσεως ἔδει μόνον ,
sections.translation: For it needs [*] now only a trifling pretext for me and the daughters remaining to give you the reception it is right you should receive . I adjure you by the gods , do not compel me to sin against you , or sin yourself .

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: εἶἑν ·
sections.translation: Well ; suppose you sacrifice the child ; what prayer will you utter , when it is done ? what will the blessing be that you will invoke upon yourself as you are slaying our daughter ? An ill returning , seeing the disgrace that speeds your going forth ? Is it right that I should pray for any luck to attend you ? Surely we should deem the gods devoid of sense ,

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: εἰ τοῖσιν αὐθένταισιν εὖ φρονήσομεν ;
sections.translation: if we harbored a kindly feeling towards murder ? Shall you embrace your children on your coming back to Argos ? No , you have no right . Will any child of yours ever face you , if you have surrendered one of them to death ? [*] Has this ever entered into your calculations , or does your one duty consist

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: μόνον διαφέρειν καὶ στρατηλατεῖν μέλει ;
sections.translation: in carrying a scepter about and marching at the head of an army ? When you might have made this fair proposal among the Argives ; Is it your wish , Achaeans , to sail for Phrygia ’ s shores ? Why then , cast lots whose daughter has to die . For that would have been a fair course for you to pursue , instead of picking out

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: σφάγιον παρασχεῖν Δαναΐδαισι παῖδα σήν ,
sections.translation: your own child for the victim and presenting her to the Danaids ; or Menelaus , as it was his concern , should have slain Hermione for her mother . As it is , I , who still am true to your bed , must lose my child ; while she , who went astray ,

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: Σπάρτῃ κομίζουσʼ , εὐτυχὴς γενήσεται .
sections.translation: will return with her daughter , and live in happiness at Sparta . If I am wrong in my words , answer me ; but if they have been fairly urged , do not still [*] slay your child , who is mine too , and you will be wise .

sections.label: Section 1209
sections.original: πιθοῦ . τὸ γάρ τοι τέκνα συνσῴζειν καλόν ,
sections.translation: Hearken to her , Agamemnon , for to join in saving your children ’ s lives is surely a noble deed ;

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: Ἀγάμεμνον · οὐδεὶς πρὸς τάδʼ ἀντερεῖ βροτῶν .
sections.translation: no one will deny this .

sections.label: Section 1211
sections.original: εἰ μὲν τὸν Ὀρφέως εἶχον , ὦ πάτερ , λόγον ,
sections.translation: If I had the eloquence of Orpheus , my father , to move the rocks by chanted spells to follow me , or to charm by speaking anyone I wished , I would have resorted to it . But as it is , I ’ ll bring my tears — the only art I know ;

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: δάκρυα παρέξω · ταῦτα γὰρ δυναίμεθʼ ἄν .
sections.translation: for that I might attempt . And about your knees , as a suppliant , I twine my limbs — these limbs your wife here bore . Do not destroy me before my time , for it is sweet to look upon the light , and do not force me to visit scenes below .

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: πρώτη σʼ ἐκάλεσα πατέρα καὶ σὺ παῖδʼ ἐμέ ·
sections.translation: I was the first to call you father , you the first to call me child ; I was the first to sit upon your knee and give and take the fond caress . And this was what you would saythen : Shall I see you , my child , living a happy prosperous life

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: ζῶσάν τε καὶ θάλλουσαν ἀξίως ἐμοῦ ;
sections.translation: in a husband ’ s home one day , in a manner worthy of myself ? And I in my turn would ask , as I hung about your beard , where I now am clinging , What then will I do for you ? Shall I be giving you a glad reception in my halls , father ,

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: πόνων τιθηνοὺς ἀποδιδοῦσά σοι τροφάς ;
sections.translation: in your old age , repaying all your anxious care in rearing me ?

sections.label: Section 1231
sections.original: τούτων ἐγὼ μὲν τῶν λόγων μνήμην ἔχω ,
sections.translation: I remember all we said , it is you who have forgotten and now would take my life . By Pelops , I entreat you spare me , by your father Atreus and my mother here , who suffers now a second time the pangs

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: νῦν δευτέραν ὠδῖνα τήνδε λαμβάνει .
sections.translation: she felt before when bearing me ! What have I to do with the marriage of Paris and Helen ? Why is his coming to prove my ruin , father ? Look upon me ; bestow one glance , one kiss , that this at least I may carry to my death

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: μνημεῖον , ἢν μὴ τοῖς ἐμοῖς πεισθῇς λόγοις .
sections.translation: as a memorial of you , though you do not heed my pleading .

sections.label: Section 1241
sections.original: ἀδελφέ , μικρὸς μὲν σύ γʼ ἐπίκουρος φίλοις ,
sections.translation: holding up the baby Orestes . Feeble ally though you are , brother , to your loved ones , yet add your tears to mine and entreat our father for your sister ’ s life ; even in babies there is a natural sense of evil .

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: ἰδοὺ σιωπῶν λίσσεταί σʼ ὅδʼ , ὦ πάτερ .
sections.translation: O father , see this speechless supplication made to you ; pity me ; have mercy on my tender years ! Yes , by your beard we two fond hearts implore your pity , the one a baby , a full-grown maid the other . By summing all my pleas in one , I will prevail in what I say :

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: τὸ φῶς τόδʼ ἀνθρώποισιν ἥδιστον βλέπειν ,
sections.translation: to gaze upon the light is man ’ s most cherished gift ; that life below is nothingness , and whoever longs for death is mad . Better live a life of woe than die a death of glory !

sections.label: Section 1253
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον Ἑλένη , διὰ σὲ καὶ τοὺς σοὺς γάμους
sections.translation: Ah , wretched Helen ! Great is the struggle that has come sons to the of Atreus and their children , thanks to you and those marriages of yours .

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.original: ἐγὼ τά τʼ οἰκτρὰ συνετός εἰμι καὶ τὰ μή ,
sections.translation: While loving my own children , I yet understand what should move my pity and what should not ; I would be a madman otherwise . It is terrible for me to bring myself to this , nor is it less terrible to refuse , daughter ; for I must do this . [*] You see the vastness of that naval army ,

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: χαλκέων θʼ ὅπλων ἄνακτες Ἑλλήνων ὅσοι ,
sections.translation: and the numbers of bronze-clad warriors from Hellas , who can neither make their way to Ilium ’ s towers nor raze the far-famed citadel of Troy , unless I offer you according to the word of Calchas the seer . [*] Some mad desire possesses the army of Hellas

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: πλεῖν ὡς τάχιστα βαρβάρων ἐπὶ χθόνα ,
sections.translation: to sail at once to the land of the barbarians , and put a stop to the rape of wives from Hellas , and they will slay my daughter in Argos as well as you and me , if I disregard the goddess ’ s commands . It is not Menelaus who has enslaved me to him , child ,

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἐπὶ τὸ κείνου βουλόμενον ἐλήλυθα ,
sections.translation: nor have I followed his wish ; no , it is Hellas , for whom I must sacrifice you whether I will or not ; to this necessity I bow my head ; for her freedom must be preserved , as far as any help of yours daughter , or mine can go ; or they , who are the sons of Hellas , must be

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: Ἕλληνας ὄντας λέκτρα συλᾶσθαι βίᾳ .
sections.translation: pillaged of their wives by barbarian robbery . Exit Agamemnon .

sections.label: Section 1276
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , ὦ ξέναι ,
sections.translation: My child ! oh , foreign women ! Alas for me , for your death ! Your father escapes , surrendering you to Hades .

sections.label: Section 1279
sections.original: οἲ ʼγώ , μᾶτερ · ταὐτὸν τόδε γὰρ
sections.translation: Alas for me , mother ! for the same

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: μέλος εἰς ἄμφω πέπτωκε τύχης ,
sections.translation: lament has fallen to both of us in our fortune . No more for me the light of day ! no more these beams of the sun ! Oh , oh ! that snow-beat glen in Phrygia and the hills of Ida ,

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: Πρίαμος ὅθι ποτὲ βρέφος ἁπαλὸν ἔβαλε
sections.translation: where Priam once exposed a tender baby , torn from his mother ’ s arms to meet a deadly doom , Paris , called the child of Ida

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: δαῖος ἐλέγετʼ ἐλέγετʼ ἐν Φρυγῶν πόλει ,
sections.translation: in the Phrygians ’ town . Would that he never had settled Alexander , the herdsman reared among the herds , beside that water crystal-clear , where are fountains

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: Νυμφᾶν κεῖνται
sections.translation: of the Nymphs and their meadow rich with blooming flowers , where hyacinths and rose-buds blow for goddesses to gather ! Here one day

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: Παλλὰς ἔμολε καὶ δολιόφρων Κύπρις
sections.translation: came Pallas and Cypris of the subtle heart , Hera too and Hermes messenger of Zeus ; Cypris , proud of the longing she causes ,

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: Κύπρις , ἃ δὲ δορὶ Παλλάς ,
sections.translation: Pallas of her prowess ; and Hera of her royal marriage with king Zeus ; to decide a hateful strife about their beauty ; but it is my death ,

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: ὄνομα μὲν φέροντα Δαναΐ-
sections.translation: maidens , bringing , it is true , glory to the Danaids , that Artemis has received as an offering , before they begin the voyage to Ilium . [*]

sections.label: Section 1313
sections.original: ὦ μᾶτερ ὦ μᾶτερ ,
sections.translation: O mother , mother ! he that begot me to this life of sorrow has gone and left me all alone .

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: ὦ δυστάλαινʼ ἐγώ , πικρὰν
sections.translation: Ah ! woe is me ! a bitter , bitter sight for me was Helen , evil Helen ! to me now doomed to bleed and die , slaughtered by an impious father !

sections.label: Section 1319
sections.original: μή μοι ναῶν χαλκεμβολάδων
sections.translation: I wish this Aulis had never received

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: πρύμνας ἅδʼ Αὐλὶς δέξασθαι
sections.translation: in its havens here the stems of their bronze-beaked ships , the fleet which was speeding them to Troy ; and would that Zeus had never breathed on the Euripus a wind to stop the expedition , [*] tempering , as he does ,

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: αὔραν ἄλλοις ἄλλαν θνατῶν
sections.translation: a different breeze to different men , so that some have joy in setting sail , and sorrow some , and others hard constraint , to make some start and others prepare and others delay !

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: ἦ πολύμοχθον ἄρʼ ἦν γένος , ἦ πολύμοχθον
sections.translation: Full of trouble then , it seems , is the race of mortals , full of trouble indeed ; and it is Fate ’ s decree that man should find distress .
